annotations.label: First-order formula

anchor.snippet: A first-order formula is built out of atomic formulas

annotations.note: BoundedFormula is the inductive type of first-order formulas (falsum/equal/rel/imp/all), with Formula the free-variable special case.

annotations.label: Sentence

anchor.snippet: A sentence is a formula in which each occurrence of a variable is in the scope of a corresponding quantifier

annotations.note: Sentence is defined as L.Formula Empty, i.e. a formula with no free variables.

annotations.label: Example formulas

anchor.snippet: Examples for formulas are

annotations.note: The formula-building framework exists, but these specific illustrative formulas are not declared in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formula defining prime numbers

anchor.snippet: satisfies the formula

annotations.note: Formula satisfaction is formalized via Realize, but no specific primality formula is given.

annotations.label: Formula defining irreducibility

anchor.snippet: similarly defines irreducibility

annotations.note: Formula satisfaction is formalized, but no specific irreducibility-defining formula appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Theory

anchor.snippet: is called a (first-order) theory

annotations.note: Theory is defined as Set L.Sentence.

annotations.label: Satisfiable theory

anchor.snippet: A theory is satisfiable if it has a model

annotations.note: IsSatisfiable is defined as the existence of a nonempty model of the theory.

annotations.label: Complete theory

anchor.snippet: A complete theory is a theory that contains every sentence or its negation

annotations.note: IsComplete states the theory is satisfiable and models each sentence or its negation (with IsMaximal the syntactic membership variant).

annotations.label: Theory of a structure

anchor.snippet: is also called the theory of that structure

annotations.note: completeTheory M is defined as the set of all sentences satisfied by M.

annotations.label: Gödel's completeness theorem (model iff consistent)

anchor.snippet: a theory has a model if and only if it is consistent

annotations.note: Mathlib's ModelTheory has no proof system/provability, so the syntactic completeness theorem is not formalized (only semantic compactness).

annotations.label: Signature / language

anchor.snippet: A signature or language is a set of non-logical symbols

annotations.note: Language bundles arity-indexed types of function and relation symbols.

annotations.label: Structure

anchor.snippet: A structure is a set

annotations.note: Structure provides funMap and RelMap interpretations of the language symbols on a carrier type.

annotations.label: Signature for ordered rings

anchor.snippet: A common signature for ordered rings

annotations.note: The ring language (0,1,+,*,-) and order language exist separately, but a combined ordered-ring signature is not a single Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Modelling a set of sentences

anchor.snippet: is said to model a set of first-order sentences

annotations.note: Theory.Model M T (notation M ⊨ T) holds when every sentence of T is realized in M.

annotations.label: Substructure

anchor.snippet: is a subset of its domain, closed under all functions in its signature

annotations.note: Substructure is a carrier set with fun_mem (ClosedUnder for every function symbol, including constants).

annotations.label: Elementary substructure

anchor.snippet: A substructure is said to be elementary if for any first-order formula

annotations.note: ElementarySubstructure (via Substructure.IsElementary) requires every formula to agree between the substructure and the ambient structure.

annotations.label: Elementary substructure preserves modelling

anchor.snippet: an elementary substructure is a model of a theory exactly when the superstructure is a model

annotations.note: theory_model_iff states S ⊨ T ↔ M ⊨ T for an elementary substructure S of M.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers vs rationals as substructures

anchor.snippet: While the field of algebraic numbers

annotations.note: This specific example comparing the algebraic numbers and rationals as substructures is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Embedding and elementary embedding

anchor.snippet: An embedding of a σ-structure

annotations.note: Embedding (in Basic) and ElementaryEmbedding (commuting with formula realization) are both defined.

annotations.label: Embedding is injective homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Every embedding is an injective homomorphism

annotations.note: Embedding.toHom realizes every embedding as a homomorphism and Embedding.injective shows it is injective.

annotations.label: Reduct and expansion

anchor.snippet: is that of a reduct of a structure to a subset of the original signature

annotations.note: LHom.reduct restricts a structure along a language map, with IsExpansionOn capturing the expansion direction.

annotations.label: Compactness theorem

anchor.snippet: The compactness theorem states that a set of sentences S is satisfiable if every finite subset of S is satisfiable

annotations.note: This theorem states a theory is satisfiable iff it is finitely satisfiable, proved via ultraproducts.

annotations.label: Compactness corollary (finite unsatisfiable subset)

anchor.snippet: every unsatisfiable first-order theory has a finite unsatisfiable subset

annotations.note: This is the immediate contrapositive of the compactness iff theorem.

annotations.label: Löwenheim–Skolem theorem

anchor.snippet: every infinite structure in a countable signature has a countable elementary substructure

annotations.note: The downward Löwenheim–Skolem theorem is proved for an arbitrary infinite cardinal κ; the countable case is κ = ℵ₀.

annotations.label: LS: countable and arbitrarily large models

anchor.snippet: any theory in a countable signature with infinite models has a countable model as well as arbitrarily large models

annotations.note: exists_large_model_of_infinite_model gives arbitrarily large models and exists_model_card_eq gives a model of any cardinality ≥ ℵ₀ (including countable).

annotations.label: Lindström's theorem

anchor.snippet: first-order logic is the most expressive logic for which both the Löwenheim–Skolem theorem and the compactness theorem hold

annotations.note: Lindström's abstract-model-theory characterization of first-order logic is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formula defining the primes

anchor.snippet: defines the subset of prime numbers

annotations.note: The definable-set framework exists, but no specific formula defining the primes is declared.

annotations.label: Formula defining the evens

anchor.snippet: defines the subset of even numbers

annotations.note: Definability is formalized (and Presburger definability of subsets of ℕ exists), but the even-numbers example itself is not a declaration.

annotations.label: Formula defining a curve in a field

anchor.snippet: defines the curve of all

annotations.note: Definable sets over a structure are formalized, but this specific curve example is not.

annotations.label: Parameter-free vs parametric definability

anchor.snippet: Both of the definitions mentioned here are parameter-free

annotations.note: Set.Definable is parameterized by a set A (parametric), and empty_definable_iff isolates the parameter-free (A = ∅) case.

annotations.label: Curve defined with a parameter

anchor.snippet: to define a curve

annotations.note: Definability-with-parameters is formalized, but this specific parametric curve example is not declared.

annotations.label: Quantifier elimination

anchor.snippet: A theory T has quantifier elimination if every first-order formula

annotations.note: Quantifier-free formulas (IsQF) and prenex forms are formalized, but quantifier elimination as a property of a theory is not defined.

annotations.label: QE implies quantifier-free definability

anchor.snippet: every set definable in a structure is definable by a quantifier-free formula over the same parameters as the original definition

annotations.note: Quantifier-free formulas and definable sets both exist, but the consequence of quantifier elimination is not stated since QE is undefined.

annotations.label: ACF has quantifier elimination

anchor.snippet: the theory of algebraically closed fields in the signature

annotations.note: The theory ACF and its completeness/categoricity are formalized, but quantifier elimination for ACF is not proved.

annotations.label: ACF formulas are Boolean combinations of polynomial equations

anchor.snippet: every formula is equivalent to a Boolean combination of equations between polynomials

annotations.note: ACF is defined, but the quantifier-elimination consequence (Boolean combinations of polynomial equations) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Model-complete theory

anchor.snippet: A theory T is called model-complete if every substructure of a model of T

annotations.note: Model-completeness is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tarski–Vaught test

anchor.snippet: called the Tarski–Vaught test

annotations.note: The Tarski–Vaught test for elementarity of a substructure (and the embedding variant) is formalized.

annotations.label: Model-completeness criterion (existential formulas)

anchor.snippet: a theory T is model-complete if and only if every first-order formula

annotations.note: Since model-completeness is not defined, its existential-formula criterion is absent.

annotations.label: Model completion and model companion

anchor.snippet: may have a model completion

annotations.note: Model completion and model companion are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite subsets are definable with parameters

anchor.snippet: is definable with parameters

annotations.note: Singletons are definable (Definable.singleton) and definability is closed under finite unions, but a single 'finite set is definable' lemma is not stated.

annotations.label: Cofinite subsets are definable

anchor.snippet: is also always definable

annotations.note: Definable sets are closed under complement (Boolean algebra) and contain singletons, giving cofinite sets, but this is not a single named result.

annotations.label: Minimal structure

anchor.snippet: is called minimal if every subset

annotations.note: Minimal structures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strongly minimal theory

anchor.snippet: A theory T is called strongly minimal if every model of T is minimal

annotations.note: Strong minimality is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ACF is strongly minimal

anchor.snippet: the theory of algebraically closed fields is strongly minimal

annotations.note: Strong minimality is undefined, so this result about ACF is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real numbers are not minimal

anchor.snippet: of real numbers is not minimal

annotations.note: Minimality is undefined, so this example is not formalized.

annotations.label: o-minimal structure

anchor.snippet: is called o-minimal if every subset

annotations.note: o-minimality is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Definable functions and constants

anchor.snippet: Functions are definable if the function graph is a definable relation

annotations.note: DefinableFun is defined exactly as 'the graph is definable', with definableFun_const giving definable constants.

annotations.label: Interpretable structures

anchor.snippet: We say that these structures are interpretable

annotations.note: First-order interpretation of one structure in another is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Undecidability transfers through interpretation

anchor.snippet: interprets another whose theory is undecidable

annotations.note: Interpretations and decidability transfer are not formalized.

annotations.label: Complete type realised by a tuple

anchor.snippet: This is called the complete (n-)type realised by

annotations.note: typeOf v is the complete type (set of formulas true at the tuple v) realised by v.

annotations.label: Automorphism yields same type

anchor.snippet: realise the same complete type over A

annotations.note: typeOf is defined and isomorphisms preserve formula realization, but the explicit 'automorphic tuples share a type' lemma is not stated.

annotations.label: Types over the real order line

anchor.snippet: satisfies the same 1-type over the empty set

annotations.note: This worked example of 1-types over the real order is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partial n-type

anchor.snippet: a (partial) n-type over A is a set of formulas

annotations.note: Only complete types are formalized (as maximal consistent theories); partial types are noted as a TODO and not defined.

annotations.label: Complete type

anchor.snippet: If p contains every such formula or its negation, then p is complete

annotations.note: CompleteType T α is a maximal consistent set of formulas over T in variables α.

annotations.label: Isolated type

anchor.snippet: then p is called isolated

annotations.note: Isolated types are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Type over integers unrealised in the reals

anchor.snippet: that is not realised in the real number line

annotations.note: This specific unrealised-type example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Type-definable set

anchor.snippet: is called type-definable over A

annotations.note: Type-definable sets are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ACF types correspond to prime ideals

anchor.snippet: corresponds to the set of prime ideals of the polynomial ring

annotations.note: The correspondence between ACF types and prime ideals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Structures realise their isolated types

anchor.snippet: every structure realises its isolated types

annotations.note: Isolated types are undefined, so this result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Atomic structure

anchor.snippet: then the structure is called atomic

annotations.note: Atomic structures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Saturated structure

anchor.snippet: A structure is called saturated if it realises every type over a parameter set

annotations.note: Saturated structures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous structure

anchor.snippet: is called (strongly) homogeneous

annotations.note: IsUltrahomogeneous (embeddings of finitely generated substructures extend to automorphisms) is a closely related notion, but the type-based homogeneity here is not directly defined.

annotations.label: Real line atomic, rationals saturated

anchor.snippet: The real number line is atomic in the language that contains only the order

annotations.note: These atomic/saturated examples require undefined notions and are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Definable subsets form a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: is a Boolean algebra

annotations.note: DefinableSet carries a BooleanAlgebra instance.

annotations.label: Stone space of n-types

anchor.snippet: This is called the Stone space of n-types over A

annotations.note: CompleteType T α is equipped with the topology generated by the clopen sets typesWith, i.e. the Stone space of types.

annotations.label: Stone space is compact; isolated types

anchor.snippet: The compactness theorem says that the Stone space is a compact topological space

annotations.note: The CompactSpace instance on CompleteType T α is proved (the isolated-points correspondence is not separately formalized).

annotations.label: Constructible topology on ACF type space

anchor.snippet: the topology on the type space is the constructible topology

annotations.note: The identification of the ACF type space with the constructible topology on prime ideals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Omitting types theorem

anchor.snippet: be a countable set of non-isolated types over the empty set

annotations.note: The omitting types theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of an atomic model

anchor.snippet: then this theory has an atomic model

annotations.note: Atomic models and their existence are not formalized.

annotations.label: Realising types in an elementary extension

anchor.snippet: Then there is an elementary extension

annotations.note: Mathlib proves every complete type is realised in some model of T, but not the stronger statement constructing an elementary extension of a given structure realizing a set of types.

annotations.label: Saturated model existence independent of ZFC

anchor.snippet: whether every theory has a saturated model is independent of the axioms of Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory

annotations.note: Saturated models and this independence result are not formalized.

annotations.label: Ultraproduct

anchor.snippet: An ultraproduct is obtained from the direct product of a set of structures

annotations.note: The ultraproduct is Filter.Product over an ultrafilter, given an L.Structure via Ultraproduct.structure.

annotations.label: Ultrapower

anchor.snippet: is known as an ultrapower

annotations.note: Ultraproducts are formalized (ultrapower is the constant-family case), but there is no dedicated 'ultrapower' definition.

annotations.label: Łoś's theorem

anchor.snippet: indexed by an index set I and U an ultrafilter on I

annotations.note: Łoś's theorem (sentence holds in the ultraproduct iff the index set of structures satisfying it is in the ultrafilter) is proved.

annotations.label: Ultraproduct of models is a model

anchor.snippet: any ultraproduct of models of a theory is itself a model of that theory

annotations.note: This follows immediately from Łoś's theorem (and is used in the compactness proof), but is not stated as a standalone lemma.

annotations.label: Keisler–Shelah theorem

anchor.snippet: If M and N are elementarily equivalent, then there is a set I and an ultrafilter U on I

annotations.note: The Keisler–Shelah isomorphic-ultrapowers theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Categorical theory (original notion)

anchor.snippet: A theory was originally called categorical if it determines a structure up to isomorphism

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the cardinal-relativized Categorical notion, not the original unrelativized 'one model up to isomorphism' notion.

annotations.label: Only finite structures are categorical

anchor.snippet: only finite structures can be described by a categorical theory

annotations.note: This consequence of the unrelativized categoricity notion is not formalized.

annotations.label: κ-categorical theory

anchor.snippet: A theory T is called κ -categorical if any two models of T that are of cardinality κ are isomorphic

annotations.note: Cardinal.Categorical κ T states all models of T of cardinality κ are isomorphic.

annotations.label: Ryll-Nardzewski theorem (ω-categoricity)

anchor.snippet: For a complete first-order theory T in a finite or countable signature the following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: The Ryll-Nardzewski characterization of ω-categoricity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dense linear orders order-isomorphic to the rationals

anchor.snippet: every countable dense linear order is order-isomorphic to the rational number line

annotations.note: iso_of_countable_dense (Cantor's theorem) shows any two countable dense linear orders without endpoints are order-isomorphic; the model-theoretic aleph0_categorical_dlo also formalizes ℵ₀-categoricity of DLO.

annotations.label: Fields are not ω-categorical

anchor.snippet: the theories of ℚ, ℝ and ℂ as fields are not

annotations.note: The non-ω-categoricity of these field theories is not formalized.

annotations.label: ω-categorical iff oligomorphic automorphism group

anchor.snippet: is ω -categorical if and only if its automorphism group is oligomorphic

annotations.note: Oligomorphic automorphism groups and this characterization are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fraïssé limits

anchor.snippet: a common source of ω -categorical theories are Fraïssé limits

annotations.note: IsFraisseLimit captures a countable ultrahomogeneous structure whose age is the given Fraïssé class.

annotations.label: Morley's categoricity theorem

anchor.snippet: is κ -categorical for some uncountable cardinal κ

annotations.note: Morley's categoricity theorem is not formalized (only the specific ACF_categorical instance exists).

annotations.label: Strongly minimal theories are uncountably categorical

anchor.snippet: complete strongly minimal theories are uncountably categorical

annotations.note: Strong minimality is undefined, so this result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Totally categorical theory

anchor.snippet: is called totally categorical

annotations.note: Total categoricity is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: λ-stable theory

anchor.snippet: of cardinality not exceeding

annotations.note: λ-stability is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stable theory

anchor.snippet: A theory is called stable if it is

annotations.note: Stable theories are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stability spectrum theorem

anchor.snippet: A fundamental result in stability theory is the stability spectrum theorem

annotations.note: The stability spectrum theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Unstable, strictly stable, superstable

anchor.snippet: A theory of the first type is called unstable

annotations.note: These stability classifications are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Main gap theorem

anchor.snippet: the Main gap theorem implies that if there is an uncountable cardinal

annotations.note: Shelah's main gap theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Morley rank

anchor.snippet: Morley rank is an important dimension notion for definable sets

annotations.note: Morley rank is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Totally transcendental theory

anchor.snippet: is called totally transcendental

annotations.note: Total transcendence is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Totally transcendental iff ω-stable (countable)

anchor.snippet: then T is totally transcendental if and only if T is

annotations.note: Both notions are undefined, so this equivalence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simple and NIP theories

anchor.snippet: stability has been decomposed into simplicity

annotations.note: Simple and NIP theories are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stable iff NIP and simple

anchor.snippet: a theory is stable if and only if it is NIP and simple

annotations.note: Stable, NIP, and simple are undefined, so this characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Homogeneous model theory

anchor.snippet: which studies the class of substructures of arbitrarily large homogeneous models

annotations.note: Homogeneous model theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quasiminimally excellent classes

anchor.snippet: quasiminimally excellent classes are those in which every definable set is either countable or co-countable

annotations.note: Quasiminimal excellence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abstract elementary classes

anchor.snippet: which are defined by a strong substructure relation generalising that of an elementary substructure

annotations.note: Abstract elementary classes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tarski's quantifier elimination results

anchor.snippet: Tarski's proofs of quantifier elimination for various algebraically interesting classes

annotations.note: Quantifier elimination for real closed/algebraically closed fields is not formalized in Mathlib's model theory.

annotations.label: Theory of finite fields is decidable (Ax)

anchor.snippet: proving that the theory of finite fields is decidable

annotations.note: Ax's theorem on the decidability of the theory of finite fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ax–Kochen theorem

anchor.snippet: the Ax–Kochen theorem

annotations.note: The Ax–Kochen theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hrushovski's geometric Mordell–Lang

anchor.snippet: proof of the geometric Mordell–Lang conjecture in all characteristics

annotations.note: Hrushovski's geometric Mordell–Lang theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pila's André–Oort theorem

anchor.snippet: to prove the André–Oort conjecture for products of Modular curves

annotations.note: Pila's André–Oort result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NIP theories and PAC-learnability

anchor.snippet: NIP theories describe exactly those definable classes that are PAC-learnable

annotations.note: The NIP/PAC-learnability correspondence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Stable theories and online learnability

anchor.snippet: stable theories correspond to online learnable classes

annotations.note: The stable/online-learnability correspondence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classical results fail for finite structures

anchor.snippet: many central results of classical model theory fail when restricted to finite structures

annotations.note: Finite model theory and the failure of compactness/Löwenheim–Skolem there are not formalized.

annotations.label: Skolem's paradox

anchor.snippet: this is known as Skolem's paradox

annotations.note: Skolem's paradox (countable models of set theory) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compactness equivalent to Boolean prime ideal theorem

anchor.snippet: equivalent over Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory without choice to the Boolean prime ideal theorem

annotations.note: The choiceless equivalence of first-order compactness with the Boolean prime ideal theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: CH implies saturated ultrapower

anchor.snippet: every countable model has an ultrapower which is saturated

annotations.note: Saturation is undefined, so this CH consequence is not formalized.

annotations.label: GCH implies saturated elementary extension

anchor.snippet: every model has a saturated elementary extension

annotations.note: Saturation is undefined, so this GCH consequence is not formalized.
